_sources_note: Jira: full data pulled (Done today, status changes today, In Product/Blocked). Slack: gdc-pod, gdc-product-led-growth, gdc-product-design-pod, gdc-high-agency read; gdc-pdmt, core-product, gdc-f2p-apps, 6amigos returned no results for today. Confluence: CQL queries run across all spaces and GDCC. Calendar: Microsoft 365 requires browser authentication — tomorrow's calendar not available; noting known recurring meetings only.

achievements.description: GDCU-8876 Implement Fixes for Site Container Update shipped to Done (Harden Wallace, 13:39 BST) — the site container UX/dev fixes that were In Product since 22 Apr are now closed and released.

Thursday release confirmed successful by QA — Shane McGrath posted at 14:56 BST: 'QA have finished verifying all tickets post release. All looks good. Also sanity checked the site in general.' Covers Bottom Nav Mobile (GDCU-8627), Sign In/Registration Modal Redesign (GDCU-8803), and related items shipped this week.

Jessica Dordevic returned from sick leave and resumed active design work — Tinder Style Offer Match (GDCU-8546) updated 15:39 BST, Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card (GDCU-8879) and Image Carousel 500x290 size (GDCU-8694) both picked up and updated 14:11 BST.
GDCU-8802 Fix Page Bloat on /uk/online-casinos cancelled — investigation ticket formally scoped out of sprint, removing backlog noise.

Design system close icon decision progressing — Ciara Carroll raised the round vs clean X close icon question at 14:55 BST in #gdc-product-led-growth; Jessica (15:44) and Miguel (15:51) both converging on the clean X as the site-wide standard.
achievements.source: Slack #gdc-product-led-growth (Ciara Carroll, Jessica Dordevic, Miguel Migneco)

achievements.description: QA automation framework expanded — Robin John published 'Gambling.com Test Automation — Setup, Progress & Framework Plan' in GDC Core Confluence, and Shane McGrath documented automated test coverage for the Global News Page.
achievements.source: Confluence — GDCC space (Robin John, Shane McGrath)

achievements.description: Backlog Grooming session held (15:30 BST) — Jessica Dordevic picked up GDCU-8879 Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card as part of sprint refinement. Tinder ticket (GDCU-8546) discussed in grooming context.
Gabriel Cornoiu flagged post-release UX issue at 12:09 BST: the 'rotate your device' message is appearing for non-mobile devices — a regression from the Thursday release that needs a fix ticket.

meeting_summaries.title: Do PP Monthly
meeting_summaries.summary: Solo monthly admin block (payroll / pulse survey). No attendees, no meeting notes published. Assumed completed at start of day.

meeting_summaries.title: Ciara/Mike Sync
meeting_summaries.summary: Weekly 1-2-1 with direct report Ciara Carroll. No Confluence notes published. Based on Jira EOD state: GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign remains Blocked/In Product — blocker not resolved during this meeting. Ciara was active in #gdc-product-led-growth throughout the day working on design system decisions (close icon, container styling), which suggests the meeting took place but GDCU-8619 escalation path remains unclear. GDCU-8801 CA/FR Oplist Redesign still In Product — sign-off not yet confirmed.
action_items_from_meeting: Confirm GDCU-8619 blocker escalation path — still Blocked at EOD; escalate to Colin Brannigan if not resolved
Sign off GDCU-8801 CA/FR Casino Oplist Redesign — In Product, Ciara waiting

meeting_summaries.title: Mike / Gab Weekly Sync
meeting_summaries.summary: Weekly sync with Gabriel Cornoiu (status was Tentative). No Confluence notes. Gabriel was active in #gdc-pod today (raised 'rotate your device' UX issue at 12:09 BST), indicating the meeting likely took place. GDCU-8807 Dynamic Content API in QA — no further status change in Jira today. GDCU-8869 PSI race condition still In Progress (Sanja Budic). GDCU-8810 Apple/Google Dev Accounts still Blocked.
action_items_from_meeting: Raise Jira ticket for 'rotate your device' UX issue flagged by Gabriel — affects non-mobile devices post-release
Get update on GDCU-8810 Apple/Google Developer Account blocker from Harden

meeting_summaries.title: Tinder Widget Planning
meeting_summaries.summary: Planning session with Chloe Christie (Zoom agenda available). Jessica Dordevic was absent at meeting time but returned later in the day. GDCU-8546 was updated at 15:39 BST by Jessica, suggesting design work resumed post-meeting. Ciara noted in #gdc-product-design-pod (9 Apr) that the Tinder ticket was discussed in a grooming context and had estimates added. No Confluence notes published from this meeting.
action_items_from_meeting: Confirm scope, acceptance criteria, and timeline for GDCU-8546 Tinder Widget with Chloe Christie
Review Jessica's updated Figma designs for the Tinder widget
Resolve transparent icon disclosure — Jessica flagged she was not informed of icon changes

meeting_summaries.title: GDCU Assemble Standup
meeting_summaries.summary: Full team daily standup. Shane McGrath posted QA sign-off in #gdc-pod at 14:56 BST immediately following standup: all Thursday release tickets verified, site sanity checked. Minor mobile bottom nav issues flagged (Novica Vukobratovic working on them). Gabriel Cornoiu's 'rotate your device' message issue was likely surfaced here. Jessica Dordevic attended despite earlier sick leave.
decisions: Thursday release verified as successful by QA — all release tickets confirmed OK
Mobile bottom nav minor issues to be resolved by Novica Vukobratovic
action_items_from_meeting: Monitor mobile bottom nav issues resolved by Novica — confirm no new tickets needed
Create Jira ticket for 'rotate your device' UX regression on non-mobile devices

meeting_summaries.title: Backlog Grooming
meeting_summaries.summary: Full team backlog grooming (organised by Chloe Christie). GDCU-8879 Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card was picked up by Jessica Dordevic and moved to In Progress during or around this session. GDCU-8546 Tinder was refined (Ciara had previously added estimates). GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign — still Blocked, no unblock path confirmed. No Confluence grooming notes published. Calendar status was 'free' — attendance confirmed by Jira activity.
decisions: GDCU-8879 Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card assigned to Jessica Dordevic and started
GDCU-8694 Image Carousel 500x290 size variant picked up by Jessica Dordevic
action_items_from_meeting: Resolve GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign blocker — not cleared in grooming, escalation required
Confirm GDCU-8512 Always On Betting Tips revised design brief following Chloe Christie's feedback

action_points.action: URGENT: Respond to Chloe Christie on NZ Instagram Headers Widget logo decision (GDCU-8840) — which logo type to use for full implementation: 'Operators - 150px - Brand Product - 474 - Transparent' (rectangles) or 'Operators - 300px - Brand Product - 474 - Square' (circles, noting not every brand has a background colour). Also: EVE logo information owed since 16 Apr. Both items still unresolved.
action_points.source: Slack #gdc-product-design-pod (Chloe Christie, 22 Apr) + carry-over from afternoon report

action_points.action: Escalate GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign blocker — still Blocked/In Product at EOD after 8+ days. Raised with Ciara in 09:30 sync but no resolution confirmed in Jira. Escalate to Colin Brannigan directly if Michael Butlin feedback is not received by tomorrow morning.
action_points.source: Jira GDCU-8619 — Blocked/In Product (Ciara Carroll) — carry-over from afternoon report

action_points.action: Product sign-off on GDCU-8714 Ace AI Full Football Coverage — QA completed 22 Apr by Shane McGrath (all 8+ leagues verified on qa16.gambling.com/ace). Mike is a named stakeholder. Straightforward sign-off required to release to production.
action_points.source: Jira GDCU-8714 — In Product — carry-over from afternoon report

action_points.action: Respond to Chloe Christie's revised brief on GDCU-8512 Always On Betting Tips Page — Mike signed off product at 10:09 but Chloe subsequently requested a revised dev-ready design using only existing site components (current offer cards, not proposed new ones). Confirm revised brief with Miguel Migneco.
action_points.source: Jira GDCU-8512 — carry-over from afternoon report

action_points.action: Raise Jira ticket for 'rotate your device' UX regression — Gabriel Cornoiu flagged in #gdc-pod (12:09 BST) that the rotate message incorrectly shows on non-mobile/desktop devices post-release. This is a product regression needing a fix ticket.

Formally confirm close icon decision for design system — Ciara, Jessica, and Miguel converged on 'clean X' icon (no circle) as the site-wide standard in #gdc-product-led-growth today (14:55–15:55 BST). This decision was made without Mike's explicit sign-off. Acknowledge and formally confirm to unblock design system documentation by Ciara.
action_points.source: Slack #gdc-product-led-growth (Ciara Carroll, Jessica Dordevic, Miguel Migneco, 23 Apr)

action_points.action: Sign off GDCU-8801 Oplist Redesign on CA/FR Casino Page — still In Product (Ciara Carroll). Diana Thurmann has approved initial mockups, Josh Smith has asked Ciara to move to dev-ready. Mike's sign-off unblocks progression.
action_points.source: Jira GDCU-8801 — In Product — carry-over from afternoon report

action_points.action: Unblock GDCU-8810 Set Up Org-Level Apple/Google Developer Accounts — still Blocked/In Progress (Harden Wallace). Get a clear status update from Harden on what external action is required (Apple/Google account provisioning).
action_points.source: Jira GDCU-8810 — Blocked/In Progress — carry-over from morning report

action_points.action: Confirm mobile bottom nav minor issues are resolved — Shane McGrath flagged 'one or two bumps to iron out with mobile bottom nav' post-release. Novica is on it, but confirm no new Jira ticket is needed and the fix is scoped.

Investigate GDCU-8792 Implement Cloudflare Bot Management — newly visible as Blocked/In QA (Sanja Budic). Not previously tracked in morning/afternoon reports. Understand the blocker before it delays the release pipeline.
action_points.source: Jira GDCU-8792 — Blocked/In QA — newly identified

action_points.action: Review Jessica Dordevic's updated Tinder Widget designs (GDCU-8546) — she returned from sick and updated the ticket at 15:39 BST. The Figma link she mentioned sending this morning should now be available. Review before next sync with Chloe Christie.
action_points.source: Jira GDCU-8546 — In Progress (Jessica Dordevic) — updated today

next_working_day.title: GDCU Assemble Standup
next_working_day.synopsis: Daily full-team standup on Friday. End-of-week tone — good opportunity to call out the successful Thursday release (QA verified), acknowledge Jessica's return, and surface any new blockers before the weekend.
discussion_topics: Thursday release confirmed successful — Bottom Nav, Sign In/Sign Up Modal, Site Container fixes all shipped
GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign — Blocked going into the weekend; escalation path needed
GDCU-8810 Apple/Google Developer Accounts — Blocked; Harden update required
Mobile bottom nav minor issues — Novica working fix; confirm status
'Rotate your device' UX regression — ticket to be raised or confirm Novica/Harden scoped it
GDCU-8792 Cloudflare Bot Management — Blocked in QA; Sanja Budic update
next_working_day.prep_notes: Come with the week's shipping scorecard: GDCU-8876 done today, Thursday release confirmed clean. Flag GDCU-8619 escalation before the weekend. Acknowledge Jessica's return. Confirm Novica's mobile bottom nav fix is in hand.

next_working_day.title: Hygiene Research Review (tentative — check calendar)
next_working_day.synopsis: 1-2-1 with Joshua Smith to review hygiene research findings. Josh published 'Identified points of improvement' in Confluence today covering 9 UX issues across online-casinos and slots pages (Show More clicks, accessibility contrast, navigation overlaps, CTA text, etc.). Several are linked to GDCU-8619 (Operator List Redesign) and have new Jira tickets (GDCU-8909, GDCU-8910, GDCU-8908, GDCU-8547).
discussion_topics: 9 UX improvement areas identified by Josh: 'Show More' click rate (34% of clicks), contrast accessibility on slots ToC boxes (GDCU-8834), bonus code field redundancy, navigation overlap issues (GDCU-8909), info icon UX (GDCU-8910), author block compression (GDCU-8908), compare feature visibility (GDCU-8547)
GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign — directly relevant to 'Show More' UX and operator score visibility findings
Prioritisation: which findings can be actioned within current sprint vs next sprint
New Jira tickets generated from research: GDCU-8547, GDCU-8834, GDCU-8908, GDCU-8909, GDCU-8910
next_working_day.prep_notes: Read Josh's Confluence page 'Identified points of improvement' before the meeting. The research directly feeds into the GDCU-8619 operator list redesign context — bring your position on the 'Show More' UX issue and operator card visibility. Several tickets are already raised (GDCU-8909, GDCU-8910). Check with Josh which items are unticket and need creating.
